titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alexis de Tocqueville Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday November 10, 2025 at 23:15

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to French author and politician Alexis de Tocqueville, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: A number of separate accessions received by the library through 2008 combine to make up the Alexis de Tocqueville Papers. Source information is recorded in the finding aid.

bioghist.head: Alexis de Tocqueville (1805-1859)
bioghist.p: Alexis de Tocqueville was a French politician and author best known for Democracy in America (1835, 1840).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alexis de Tocqueville Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: Uncat MS Vault 654, Uncat MSS 324, Uncat MSS 1138, Uncat MS Vault File

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by a unique call number and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Most folder titles appearing in the contents list below were transcribed from the recto or verso of the artwork. Otherwise, folder titles were supplied by staff during processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to French author and politician Alexis de Tocqueville, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection.

The collection primarily consists of letters from or to Tocqueville. Correspondents include John Quincy Adams, George Bancroft, Edward Everett, Léonard Faucher, Émile de Girardin, Michel Lévy, William H. Prescott, Jeanne Françoise Julie Adélaïde Bernard Récamier, Jean-Charles Rivet, Theodore Sedgwick, Jared Sparks, Charles Sumner, George Sumner, and others. Also included is a small amount of material related to Tocqueville's father Hervé de Tocqueville and brother Hippolyte des Tocqueville, and a letter book of British economist Nassau William Senior.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Alexis de Tocqueville Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Purchased from Michel Brisebois Libraire on the Louis Mayer Rabinowitz Memorial Fund, 1990.

did.unittitle: Tocqueville, Hippolyte de. Autograph letter, signed, to the President de la Commission des Tabacs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

otherfindaid.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault 654

custodhist.p: Purchased from Autographes on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1992.

did.unittitle: Tocqueville, Alexis de. Thirty autograph letters, signed, to Jean-Charles Rivet
Ampère, J.-J. (Jean Jacques). Nine autograph letters, signed, to Jean-Charles Rivet
Corcelle, Francisque de. Autograph letter, signed, to Jean-Charles Rivet
Lanjuinais, Victor. Ten autograph letters, signed, to Jean-Charles Rivet
